title: Hindu Mythology and Epics
description: A 50-question round drawn from the Ramayana, Mahabharata, Puranic stories, and traditional mythology.

questions.prompt: Which sage gave Kunti the mantra by which she could invoke gods and bear children?
questions.hint: He is known for both wisdom and a famously short temper.

questions.prompt: Who was Karna's foster father?
questions.hint: He was a charioteer in the court of Hastinapura.

questions.prompt: What was the name of Arjuna's conch in the Mahabharata?
questions.hint: Krishna's was Panchajanya.

questions.prompt: Bhima was regarded as the son of which deity?
questions.hint: This deity represents wind and life-breath.

questions.prompt: Nakula and Sahadeva were born through the grace of which divine twins?
questions.hint: They are known as the heavenly physicians.

questions.prompt: Who was Draupadi's brother, born from the sacrificial fire to kill Drona?
questions.hint: He later became commander of the Pandava army.

questions.prompt: Which kingdom was ruled by Jarasandha?
questions.hint: Its capital was Girivraja, in present-day Bihar.

questions.prompt: Which Pandava killed Jarasandha in a wrestling duel?
questions.hint: He was the strongest of the five brothers.

questions.prompt: Which brother of Ravana joined Rama in the Ramayana war?
questions.hint: He later became ruler of Lanka.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the Ramcharitmanas?
questions.hint: He composed it in Awadhi, not Sanskrit.

questions.prompt: From which mountain was Hanuman asked to fetch the Sanjeevani herb?
questions.hint: He lifted the entire mountain when he could not identify the herb.

questions.prompt: Who was Shakuni's sister?
questions.hint: She married the blind king Dhritarashtra.

questions.prompt: What were Karna's natural armour and earrings called together?
questions.hint: Indra obtained them from him in disguise.

questions.prompt: Who served as the military tutor of the Kuru princes?
questions.hint: His son was Ashwatthama.

questions.prompt: Who served as Karna's charioteer on the final day of his battle with Arjuna?
questions.hint: He was the king of Madra.

questions.prompt: What was the name of the one-use celestial weapon given by Indra to Karna?
questions.hint: He used it against Ghatotkacha.

questions.prompt: What was Bhishma's name before he took his terrible vow?
questions.hint: He was the son of Shantanu and Ganga.

questions.prompt: Which river goddess was Bhishma's mother?
questions.hint: She is worshipped across northern India.

questions.prompt: Who was the father of Jatayu according to the Ramayana tradition?
questions.hint: He is also linked to the sun god's charioteer line.

questions.prompt: Which vanara was considered the son of the wind god?
questions.hint: He leapt across the ocean to Lanka.

questions.prompt: What was the better-known battlefield name of Ravana's son Meghnada?
questions.hint: It means 'conqueror of Indra'.

questions.prompt: Who broke Shiva's bow to win Sita's hand?
questions.hint: He did so at Janaka's court in Mithila.

questions.prompt: Which sage officiated the Putrakameshti yajna for King Dasharatha?
questions.hint: He had been brought to Ayodhya from Anga.

questions.prompt: What was the island stronghold ruled by Ravana called?
questions.hint: It lay across the sea from southern Bharat.

questions.prompt: According to later tradition, what was Valmiki's name before he became a sage?
questions.hint: He is said to have once lived as a forest robber.

questions.prompt: Which Pandava married Hidimba?
questions.hint: Their son was Ghatotkacha.

questions.prompt: Which guru cursed Karna that he would forget the use of a key weapon at the crucial moment?
questions.hint: Karna had approached him disguised as a Brahmin.

questions.prompt: Who was the father of Prahlada?
questions.hint: He was a demon king slain by Vishnu's man-lion form.

questions.prompt: Which avatar of Vishnu killed Hiranyaksha?
questions.hint: This avatar took the form of a boar.

questions.prompt: Which avatar of Vishnu appeared as a man-lion?
questions.hint: He emerged from a pillar.

questions.prompt: Which mountain was used as the churning rod in Samudra Manthan?
questions.hint: It was steadied by Vishnu in another form.

questions.prompt: Which serpent was used as the rope in Samudra Manthan?
questions.hint: Its many heads became central to the tug-of-war between devas and asuras.

questions.prompt: Which deity drank the halahala poison produced during Samudra Manthan?
questions.hint: That act gave him the name Neelkanth.

questions.prompt: Which jewel obtained during Samudra Manthan is associated with Vishnu?
questions.hint: It is worn on his chest.

questions.prompt: Who is traditionally credited with composing the Mahabharata?
questions.hint: He was the son of Parashara and Satyavati.

questions.prompt: Who served as the scribe of the Mahabharata according to tradition?
questions.hint: He agreed to write on the condition that Vyasa recite without pause.

questions.prompt: Which king was the father of Rama?
questions.hint: He ruled Ayodhya of the Ikshvaku line.

questions.prompt: Which brother of Rama ruled as regent while placing Rama's sandals on the throne?
questions.hint: He refused to usurp the kingdom.

questions.prompt: Which sister of Ravana approached Rama and Lakshmana in the Panchavati forest?
questions.hint: Lakshmana disfigured her.

questions.prompt: What is the traditional name of the bridge built to Lanka by the vanara army?
questions.hint: It is associated in popular tradition with the chain of shoals near Rameswaram.

questions.prompt: Which warrior is called Gudakesha in the Mahabharata?
questions.hint: He was Krishna's chariotee.

questions.prompt: Which warrior is called Radheya in the Mahabharata?
questions.hint: The name comes from his foster mother.

questions.prompt: Who was the son of Arjuna and Subhadra?
questions.hint: He died trapped inside the Chakravyuha.

questions.prompt: From whom did Abhimanyu learn the way into the Chakravyuha while still in the womb, according to tradition?
questions.hint: He heard the explanation while his father spoke to Subhadra.

questions.prompt: Which king sheltered the Pandavas during their final year of exile in disguise?
questions.hint: His kingdom was Matsya.

questions.prompt: What was Yudhishthira's disguise-name in Virata's court?
questions.hint: He posed as a courtier skilled in dice and counsel.

questions.prompt: What was Arjuna's disguise-name in Virata's court?
questions.hint: He taught dance and music while living as a eunuch.

questions.prompt: What was Bhima's disguise-name in Virata's court?
questions.hint: He worked in the royal kitchens.

questions.prompt: What was Krishna's capital city after he left Mathura?
questions.hint: It was built on the western coast.

questions.prompt: Who killed Kamsa?
questions.hint: The act ended the tyrant's rule over Mathura.
